title: pinguicula michoacana
intro: Pinguicula michoacana, also known as the Butterwort or Mexican Butterwort, is a rare and carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula michoacana can be found in some regions, typically in the form of teas, infusions, or extracts, although its use should be approached with caution due to limited scientific research and potential toxicity.
